A 10-Day Interactive ELA/ESL Unit

Comprehensive Teacher Guide & Presentation

Unit Objectives & Targets

Target Grammar

Past Simple vs. Past Continuous

Target Vocabulary

extinguishedmaterializedmagnificentcaptivatedhastecompassiondignitydiligenceencounteredperseverancehesitationmajesticremorseful

CCSS Standards

RL.6.1L.6.1
Cinderella Slide 2 of 75

Day 1

Archetype & Media Launch

Phase: Engage Media: Video-1 (Animated Reading)
Cinderella Slide 3 of 75

Media: Archetype & Media Launch

15 Mins
Day 1 • Archetype & Media Launch
Cinderella Slide 4 of 75

Archetype & Media Launch

15 Mins
Day 1 • Archetype & Media Launch
Visual: Split screen. Left: Image of Cinderella. Right: Image of Stepmother. Definition of Archetype. Video: Cinderella clip. Prompt: 'Watch closely. Identify the Heroine and the Oppressor'.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Class, look at the screen. We see two characters from our story. One is the Heroine, and one is the Oppressor. Let's watch this short video clip and observe their actions and expressions.' Materials: Slide, Video-1. Formative Checkpoint: Ask students to point to the Oppressor. Expected answer: The Stepmother. Fast Finisher: Write down one adjective describing Cinderella's expression."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT identify the 'Heroine' and 'Oppressor' archetypes in Cinderella using visual evidence. Success Criteria: Completed T-chart identifying Cinderella as the Heroine and the Stepmother as the Oppressor with 1 visual detail each.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.6.2 - (Visual Literacy)

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (kind, stern, cold, smile). Sentence frame: 'I see the ________ because she has a ________.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'The stepmother represents the oppressor because...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'How does the visual contrast between Cinderella and her stepmother establish their archetypal roles?'

Differentiation (Extension)

Provide printed character cards with simplified visual cues for students needing extra support. Advanced students can write a sentence comparing the two characters' expressions.

Cinderella Slide 5 of 75
👁️ TEACHER MODELING

Visual Symbolism

10 Mins
Day 1 • Archetype & Media Launch
Visual: Large illustration of the Fireplace/Cinders. Prompt: 'Why does she sit here? Is the fire just for warmth, or does it represent something inside her?' Timer: 3-Minute Discussion.
⏱️ CLASSRROM PACING TIMER
03:00

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Now, let's look at the fireplace where Cinderella sits. Is it just a place to get warm, or does it represent her internal hope?' Materials: Slide, Discussion Worksheet. Formative Checkpoint: Turn-and-talk check. Expected answer: It represents her hope burning inside. Fast Finisher: Draw a small symbol of hope.
Note: Run the dynamic [3-MINUTE TIMER ANIMATION HERE] on screen to pace socratic talks."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT analyze the fireplace as a symbol of Cinderella's internal hope. Success Criteria: Partner discussion notes explaining what the fireplace represents.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.6.2

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (fire, warm, hope, dark). Sentence frame: 'The fire represents ________.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'Cinderella sits by the fireplace because...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'In what ways does the fireplace serve as a metaphor for Cinderella's internal state?'

Differentiation (Extension)

Provide a graphic organizer with a pre-drawn fire and lines for writing. Advanced students can write a short paragraph using the word 'extinguished'.

Cinderella Slide 6 of 75
🤝 TOGETHER

Character Profiling

20 Mins
Day 1 • Archetype & Media Launch
Visual: Profile Card (Name/Core Desire/Obstacle). Text: Trait Adjectives (e.g., Resilient, Cruel). Task: 'Create a profile card for the protagonist using text evidence.'

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'You will now create a profile card for Cinderella. Use the text from Chapter 1 to find her core desire and the obstacles she faces.' Materials: Chapter 1 text, Profile Card template. Formative Checkpoint: Check if students have written a core desire. Expected answer: To have a better life / be happy. Fast Finisher: Create a profile card for the stepsisters."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT create a character profile card using explicit text evidence. Success Criteria: Completed profile card with name, core desire, obstacle, and 1 quote.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.RL.6.1

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (kind, work, dress, hope). Sentence frame: 'Cinderella wants ________ but her stepmother ________.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'Cinderella's main obstacle is...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Analyze how Cinderella's internal traits help her cope with her external obstacles.'

Differentiation (Extension)

Provide a partially filled profile card for struggling readers. Advanced students can compare Cinderella's traits with her stepsisters' traits.

Cinderella Slide 7 of 75

Prediction Ticket

05 Mins
Day 1 • Archetype & Media Launch
Visual: Image of a spark or a wand. Sentence Starter: 'I predict the catalyst for change will be ________ because she wants ________.'

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Before we leave, write your prediction on the exit ticket. What will change Cinderella's life?' Materials: Exit Ticket slips. Formative Checkpoint: Collect and review tickets. Expected answer: A fairy godmother or an invitation. Fast Finisher: Write one question you have about the next chapter."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT predict the catalyst for change based on character desire. Success Criteria: Completed exit ticket with a logical prediction.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.RL.6.1

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (magic, ball, prince, help). Sentence frame: 'I predict ________ will help her.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'The catalyst for change will likely be...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Formulate a hypothesis about how Cinderella's situation will shift based on her desires.'

Differentiation (Extension)

Allow oral predictions for WIDA 1 students. Provide a choice of three pre-written predictions for students to select and justify.

Cinderella Slide 8 of 75

Collaborative Workshop Practice

15 Mins
Day 3 • Structural Pacing
Collaborative prompt:
- Partner or small-group ELA activity
- Cite textual evidence directly!

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: "Work with your partner. Spend 10 minutes discussing and sorting these answers..." Materials: Graphic organizer worksheet. Checkpoint: Circulate and verify student responses."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT apply analyzing skills in groups of 2-3. Success Criteria: Filled graphic organizers with text citations.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.7.1 - Productive student cooperation

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Vocabulary word wall cards.

Differentiation (Extension)

Group students strategically by complementary WIDA bands.

Independent Evaluation & Exit Ticket

10 Mins
Day 3 • Structural Pacing
Independent Task (Exit Ticket):
- Complete the synthesis task
- Write 3 sentences supporting your viewpoint.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: "This is your final independent exit ticket. Write silently for 10 minutes..." Materials: Print workbook slips. Checkpoint: Collect slips to grade for lesson mastery."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT demonstrate independent mastery. Success Criteria: Submitted exit ticket answering ELA skill questions.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.W.7.4 - Standard ELA evaluation

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word banks. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph templates.

Differentiation (Extension)

Extend text guidelines for high-achievers.

Day 4

Conflict Reflection

Phase: Explain Media: Illustrated Story / Chapter Images
Cinderella Slide 18 of 75

Conflict Reflection

10 Mins
Day 4 • Conflict Reflection
Visual: Image of Cinderella looking at her reflection in a mirror vs. Cinderella sweeping the floor while her stepsisters laugh. Text: 'Internal Conflict: A struggle inside a character's mind (e.g., sadness, hope). External Conflict: A struggle with an outside force (e.g., stepmother's cruelty). Prompt: Have you ever smiled when you wanted to scream?'

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Class, look at the images on the slide. Cinderella experiences both internal and external conflicts. When she smiles despite her sadness, that is an internal struggle. When her stepmother forbids her from going to the ball, that is an external struggle.' Materials: Slide, Graphic Organizer. Formative Checkpoint: Ask students to turn and talk about which conflict is harder"

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT analyze internal vs. external conflict in Cinderella. Success Criteria: Completed conflict identification chart with 1 internal and 1 external conflict.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.RL.6.1 - (Conflict Analysis)

Cinderella Slide 19 of 75

Day 5

Grammar Isolation

Phase: Explain Media: Video-1 & MP3 (Audio Story)
Cinderella Slide 20 of 75

Media: The Freeze-Frame Hook

10 Mins
Day 5 • Grammar Isolation
Cinderella Slide 21 of 75

The Freeze-Frame Hook

10 Mins
Day 5 • Grammar Isolation
🎬 [FREEZE-FRAME MOVIE VISUAL]
Background continuous action (Past Continuous) vs Interrupting sudden action (Simple Past).
Examples:
1. Cinderella was scrubbing (background past cont.) when the messenger arrived (interrupting simple past).
2. While she was baking bread, her dress caught a ember.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: "Good morning class! Let's look at the central question on our Slide. What do you think about..." Materials: Warm-up slide. Checkpoint: Ask students for quick hand signals."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT activate background knowledge related to Day 5 ELA themes. Success Criteria: Participation in active vocabulary warming tasks.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.RL.7.1 - Text-based introductions

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Sentence frames and word lists provided.

Differentiation (Extension)

Offer visual alternatives.

Cinderella Slide 22 of 75
👁️ TEACHER MODELING

Teacher Script & Instructions

Checkpoint: Correct physically if any student misidentifies past continuous."

SWBAT comprehend central ELA/ESL focuses of Day 5. Success Criteria: Note-taking or highlighting target text elements.

Day 6

Reality vs. Fiction

Phase: Elaborate Media: Royal Invitation Realia
Cinderella Slide 26 of 75

📜 Day 6 Realia Artifact: Royal Proclamation

Fairy Tales Academy • Social Stratification Analysis

💎 ROYAL PROCLAMATION BY HIS MAJESTY THE KING 💎

YOU ARE CORDIALLY INVITED TO THE MIDNIGHT CELEBRATION BALL

To celebrate the return of our Crown Prince and discover a future princess.

DATE: Friday, the Eleventh Evening of the Autumn Solstice
TIME: At Seven of the Clock until the Midnight Toll
LOCATION: The Imperial Palace Ballroom

GUEST ACCESS RULES:

  • Gentry & Nobility: Access to VIP balconies. Silver identification required.
  • Merchants & Tradesmen: Main courtyard floor level.
  • Farmers & Working Servants: Restricted to outer garden observation unless presenting an official gold gentry stamp certificate.
  • ADMISSION CONSTRAINT: All invitees must wear certified formal silk attire. Threadbare linens are strictly forbidden.

Court Chancellor Sterling, Seal Keeper of the Realm

Cinderella Slide 27 of 75

Realia Analysis

15 Mins
Day 6 • Reality vs. Fiction
📜 [REALIA ARTIFACT: ROYAL INVITATION]
Who is invited: ________________
When is the ball: Friday of the Autumn Solstice
Where is it located: The Imperial Palace Ballroom
GUEST ACCESS RULES:
- Nobility: Balcony VIP pass.
- Servant classes: Restricted to outer garden observation.
ADMISSION CONSTRAINT: Certified silk attire only. Threadbare linens forbidden.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: "Look at this historical proclamation artifact. Let us analyze who gets access, when, and where. This shows true power stratification!"
Materials: Royal Invitation Slide Document.
Checkpoint: Ask a student to locate the specific clothing rule constraint."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT apply analyzing skills in groups of 2-3. Success Criteria: Filled graphic organizers with text citations.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.7.1 - Productive student cooperation

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Vocabulary word wall cards.

Differentiation (Extension)

Group students strategically by complementary WIDA bands.

Day 7

Perspective Flip

Phase: Elaborate Media: Illustrated Story / Chapter Images
Cinderella Slide 32 of 75

Perspective Flip

10 Mins
Day 7 • Perspective Flip
Perspective Flip: Is the Stepmother truly evil, or is she remorseful and scared? Look at her cold eyes. Let's explore her point of view.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Class, today we are looking at perspective. We know the stepmother was stern with cold eyes. But later, she was remorseful. Let's think about her silent regrets.' Materials: Slide, Graphic Organizer. Formative Check: Ask students to name one reason the stepmother might feel remorseful. Expected answer: Because she was cruel to Cinderella. Fast Finisher: Write one question you would ask the stepmother."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT analyze character perspectives and identify the concept of an unreliable narrator. Success Criteria: Completed perspective comparison chart.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.7.1 - (Discussion)

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (remorseful, stern, cold, cruel). Frame: 'The stepmother was ______.' WIDA Level 3: 'The stepmother felt remorseful because ______.' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Although she acted with cruelty, her later actions suggest...'

Differentiation (Extension)

Provide visual cards showing the stepmother's expressions (stern vs. remorseful).

Cinderella Slide 33 of 75
👁️ TEACHER MODELING

Twisted Tale Synopsis

15 Mins
Day 7 • Perspective Flip
The Stepmother's Silent Regrets: She watched Cinderella with the prince. Her heart was heavy. She remembered her own harsh words. She was feeling remorseful for her past cruelty.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Let's read this short text. We see she was feeling remorseful.' Materials: Text handout. Formative Check: Have students highlight the word 'remorseful'. Fast Finisher: Find a past continuous verb in the text."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT read and analyze a short text about the stepmother's perspective. Success Criteria: Highlighted text showing remorseful feelings.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.7.1

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Highlight key words. WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'She was feeling remorseful when...' WIDA Levels 4-5: Analyze how the shift from stern to remorseful happens.

Differentiation (Extension)

Audio support for reading.

Cinderella Slide 34 of 75
🤝 TOGETHER

Role Play Scenarios

15 Mins
Day 7 • Perspective Flip
Role Play: Scenario 1: The stepmother is begging for forgiveness. Scenario 2: Cinderella shows compassion and dignity. Use words: remorseful, compassion, dignity, hesitation.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'With your partner, act out the scene where the stepmother begs for forgiveness. Cinderella, show compassion and dignity without hesitation!' Materials: Scenario cards. Formative Check: Listen for the words 'remorseful' and 'compassion'. Fast Finisher: Write down the dialogue you just spoke."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT act out a scene between Cinderella and her stepmother using target vocabulary. Success Criteria: Role-play performance using at least two target vocabulary words.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.7.1

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Simple script: 'I am remorseful.' 'I forgive you with compassion.' WIDA Level 3: 'I am remorseful for my cruelty.' 'I accept your apology with dignity.' WIDA Levels 4-5: Create original dialogue using past continuous.

Differentiation (Extension)

Pair WIDA 1-2 students with stronger speakers.

Cinderella Slide 35 of 75

Socratic Debrief

10 Mins
Day 7 • Perspective Flip
Socratic Debrief: Is anyone truly evil, or just misunderstood? Did the stepmother show real remorse? Use evidence from Chapter 6 and 10.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: 'Let's discuss. Did the stepmother show real remorse when she arrived at the palace?' Materials: Discussion rubric. Formative Check: Ensure every student contributes or writes a response. Fast Finisher: Write a summary of the debate."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT debate character motivations using text evidence. Success Criteria: One spoken contribution with evidence.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.SL.7.1

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: 'I think she is ______.' WIDA Level 3: 'She showed remorse because she ______.' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Her actions in Chapter 10 demonstrate that...'

Differentiation (Extension)

Allow written responses instead of speaking for selective

Cinderella Slide 36 of 75

Independent Evaluation & Exit Ticket

10 Mins
Day 8 • Story Arc Review
Independent Task (Exit Ticket):
- Complete the synthesis task
- Write 3 sentences supporting your viewpoint.

Explicit Teaching Scenario

Teacher Script & Instructions

"Say: "This is your final independent exit ticket. Write silently for 10 minutes..." Materials: Print workbook slips. Checkpoint: Collect slips to grade for lesson mastery."

Pedagogical Purpose

SWBAT demonstrate independent mastery. Success Criteria: Submitted exit ticket answering ELA skill questions.

Standards Alignment

CCSS.W.7.4 - Standard ELA evaluation

Scaffolding (Support)

WIDA Levels 1-2: Word banks. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph templates.

Differentiation (Extension)

Extend text guidelines for high-achievers.

Cinderella Slide 54 of 75

Formal Assessment

20 Questions

Cinderella Slide 55 of 75

Question 1

Vocabulary MCQ
In Chapter 3, what is the meaning of the word 'materialized' when the fairy godmother appeared?
A. Spoke loudly
B. Appeared suddenly from a glow
C. Ran away quickly
D. Disappeared slowly

Explanation

The text states that 'a kind old woman with a warm, comforting smile materialized from the glow,' meaning she suddenly appeared or took physical form.
Cinderella Slide 56 of 75

Question 2

Vocabulary MCQ
In Chapter 2, how did the stepmother's sharp voice affect Cinderella's spark of hope?
A. It made it bright
B. It grew stronger
C. It changed its color
D. It extinguished it

Explanation

The text states that 'Cinderella’s heart fluttered with a spark of hope, but her stepmother’s sharp voice quickly extinguished it,' meaning her hope was put out or ended.
Cinderella Slide 57 of 75

Question 3

Comprehension MCQ
Why did Cinderella run away from the grand ball in Chapter 4?
A. She lost her dress
B. Her stepsisters found her
C. The clock struck midnight, reminding her of the warning
D. She did not like the prince

Explanation

The text states that 'the deep chime of the clock striking midnight echoed through the hall, reminding her of the warning. "I must go!" she exclaimed, pulling away...'
Cinderella Slide 58 of 75

Question 4

Comprehension MCQ
What did Cinderella do when her stepmother and stepsisters begged for forgiveness in Chapter 6?
A. She forgave them without hesitation
B. She refused to speak to them
C. She made them do chores
D. She ordered them to leave the palace

Explanation

The text states: 'Showing her true character, Cinderella forgave them without hesitation, as her heart held no room for anger.'
Cinderella Slide 59 of 75

Question 5

Grammar Fill in the blank
While Cinderella ______ the dusty floors, she was imagining the beautiful music of the ball.
A. was sweeping
B. is sweeping
C. swept
D. sweeps

Explanation

We use the past continuous 'was sweeping' to describe an ongoing background action in the past while another continuous state or action was occurring.
Cinderella Slide 60 of 75

Question 6

Grammar Fill in the blank
Cinderella ______ quietly by the warm fireplace when she whispered her secret wishes to the stars.
A. is sitting
B. sat
C. sits
D. was sitting

Explanation

'Was sitting' is the past continuous, describing the ongoing background action that was happening when she whispered (past simple) her wishes.
Cinderella Slide 61 of 75

Question 7

Grammar Fill in the blank
Suddenly, the clock struck midnight while Cinderella ______ with the prince.
A. dances
B. is dancing
C. was dancing
D. danced

Explanation

The past continuous 'was dancing' describes the ongoing activity that was interrupted by the sudden event of the clock striking (past simple).
Cinderella Slide 62 of 75

Question 8

Comprehension True or False
Cinderella refused to let the palace servants attend the grand ball she hosted in Chapter 8.
True
False

Explanation

The text states that Cinderella 'even made sure the palace servants were invited to join the celebration, sharing a dance with them.'
Cinderella Slide 63 of 75

Question 9

Comprehension True or False
Cinderella personally became a teacher to a young village girl named Lily.
True
False

Explanation

Chapter 9 states: 'Cinderella decided to personally become her teacher. She visited Lily’s small home each week, bringing books and endless patience.'
Cinderella Slide 64 of 75

Question 10

Comprehension True or False
The prince presented Cinderella with a single, perfect glass slipper encased within a golden frame as a birthday surprise.
True
False

Explanation

In Chapter 10, the prince presented his gift: 'a single, perfect glass slipper, elegantly encased within a golden frame.'
Cinderella Slide 65 of 75

Question 11

Vocabulary Match two lists
Match each target vocabulary word from the story to its correct definition.
Drag from the left items to the right items to match them.
extinguished
materialized
magnificent
appeared suddenly from a glow
extremely beautiful and grand
ended or put out (like a spark of hope)

Answer

extinguished matches ended or put out (like a spark of hope); materialized matches appeared suddenly from a glow; magnificent matches extremely beautiful and grand

Explanation

These definitions directly correspond to how the words 'extinguished', 'materialized', and 'magnificent' are used in Chapters 2, 3, and 6 of the story.
Cinderella Slide 66 of 75

Question 12

Vocabulary Match two lists
Match each target vocabulary word from the story to its correct definition.
Drag from the left items to the right items to match them.
haste
compassion
diligence
kindness and caring for those who suffer
careful and persistent work or effort
speed or hurry in action

Answer

haste matches speed or hurry in action; compassion matches kindness and caring for those who suffer; diligence matches careful and persistent work or effort

Explanation

These definitions reflect the meanings of 'haste' (Chapter 4), 'compassion' (Chapter 7), and 'diligence' (Chapter 9) as demonstrated by the characters' actions.
Cinderella Slide 67 of 75

Question 13

Vocabulary Match two lists
Match each target vocabulary word from the story to its correct definition.
Drag from the left items to the right items to match them.
remorseful
perseverance
hesitation
continued effort to do something despite difficulties
a state of doubting or waiting before acting
feeling deep regret or guilt for past actions

Answer

remorseful matches feeling deep regret or guilt for past actions; perseverance matches continued effort to do something despite difficulties; hesitation matches a state of doubting or waiting before acting

Explanation

These definitions match 'remorseful' (Chapter 10), 'perseverance' (Chapter 10), and 'hesitation' (Chapter 6) based on the events and themes of the story.
Cinderella Slide 68 of 75

Question 14

Plot Order events or sequences
Put the following events from Chapters 1 to 4 of the story in the correct chronological order.
Drag and drop the items to reorder them correctly.
☰ A. Cinderella dances with the prince at the palace.
☰ B. A fairy godmother materializes in the garden and transforms Cinderella's dress.
☰ C. A royal messenger arrives with an invitation to a grand ball.
☰ D. Cinderella runs away in haste and loses a glass slipper on the staircase.

Explanation

The story progresses from the invitation (Chapter 2), to the magic transformation (Chapter 3), the dance (Chapter 4), and finally the escape where she loses her slipper (Chapter 4).
Cinderella Slide 69 of 75

Question 15

Plot Order events or sequences
Put the following events from Chapters 5 to 10 of the story in the correct chronological order.
Drag and drop the items to reorder them correctly.
☰ A. Cinderella teaches a young village girl named Lily.
☰ B. The prince searches the kingdom and Cinderella tries on the glass slipper.
☰ C. The prince surprises Cinderella with her stepmother, stepsisters, and a framed glass slipper for her birthday.
☰ D. Cinderella forgives her stepmother and stepsisters without hesitation.

Explanation

The events follow the search for the slipper (Chapter 5), the forgiveness at the palace (Chapter 6), teaching Lily (Chapter 9), and the birthday surprise (Chapter 10).
Cinderella Slide 70 of 75

Question 16

Writing Written
Write a paragraph explaining how Cinderella demonstrated perseverance and compassion throughout the story. Use at least two specific examples from the text (such as her daily chores, her treatment of her stepsisters, or her work with Lily) to support your answer.

Answer

A successful response will explain that Cinderella showed perseverance by working tirelessly on her chores without losing hope, and compassion by forgiving her stepmother and stepsisters without hesitation and teaching Lily, a young village girl.

Explanation

This prompt requires students to analyze character traits using specific textual evidence from Chapters 1, 6, and 9, aligning with CCSS RL.6.1.
Cinderella Slide 71 of 75

Question 17

Writing Written
Imagine you are the prince. Write a short journal entry describing the moment you first met Cinderella at the ball and when she suddenly ran away. In your writing, use both the Past Simple and Past Continuous to describe the actions (for example, what the guests were doing when she entered, and what you did when she ran away).

Answer

A successful response will adopt the prince's perspective and correctly use Past Simple (e.g., 'I walked', 'she ran') and Past Continuous (e.g., 'the guests were dancing', 'the clock was striking') to describe the scene.

Explanation

This prompt assesses the target grammar (Past Simple vs. Past Continuous) in a creative writing context based on Chapter 4.
Cinderella Slide 72 of 75

Question 18

Writing Written
Write a paragraph describing the scene in Chapter 9 where Cinderella and the prince encountered the family with the stuck wagon. Explain how this event showed her dignity and willingness to help, and how it helped her understand the everyday struggles of the people.

Answer

A successful response will describe how Cinderella insisted on stopping to help push the heavy wagon out of the mud, working alongside the prince and travelers, which showed her humble dignity and deepened her connection to her people.

Explanation

This prompt tests comprehension of Chapter 9 and requires students to write about character actions and their thematic meaning.
Cinderella Slide 73 of 75

Question 19

Writing Written
Analyze the change in the stepmother and stepsisters by the end of the story. Write a paragraph explaining why they felt remorseful when they arrived at the palace for Cinderella's birthday, and how Cinderella's reaction reflected her character.

Answer

A successful response will explain that they felt remorseful for their past cruelty, and Cinderella's open-hearted, forgiving greeting showed her enduring kindness, lack of anger, and grace.

Explanation

This prompt focuses on character transformation and relationships in Chapter 10, requiring textual analysis of the reconciliation scene.
Cinderella Slide 74 of 75

Question 20

Writing Written
Write a paragraph explaining the significance of the framed glass slipper that the prince gave to Cinderella as a birthday gift in Chapter 10. Why was this object so important to their story, and what did it symbolize for their legacy?

Answer

A successful response will explain that the framed glass slipper symbolized the magical night they first met, her journey from a hard life to a princess, and her legacy of perseverance, forgiveness, and hope.

Explanation

This prompt asks students to analyze symbolism and theme based on the final chapter of the story.
Cinderella Slide 75 of 75
Slide 1 of 75

Teacher Guide

📋 Unit Overview

This 10-day unit focuses on the fairy tale "Cinderella", integrating ELA skills with ESL support. Students will explore the narrative arc, analyze characters, and master target grammar (Past Simple vs. Past Continuous) and vocabulary.

🎯 Learning Objectives

1

Analyze the narrative structure and key plot points of "Cinderella".

2

Apply target grammar (Past Simple vs. Past Continuous) in both oral and written contexts.

3

Demonstrate mastery of unit vocabulary through various comprehension activities.

4

Compare and contrast different perspectives and non-fiction connections related to the story.

📅 10-Day Didactic Flow

1

Archetype & Media Launch

Engage

Video-1 (Animated Reading)

Archetype & Media Launch (15 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Class, look at the screen. We see two characters from our story. One is the Heroine, and one is the Oppressor. Let's watch this short video clip and observe their actions and expressions.' Materials: Slide, Video-1. Formative Checkpoint: Ask students to point to the Oppressor. Expected answer: The Stepmother. Fast Finisher: Write down one adjective describing Cinderella's expression."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT identify the 'Heroine' and 'Oppressor' archetypes in Cinderella using visual evidence. Success Criteria: Completed T-chart identifying Cinderella as the Heroine and the Stepmother as the Oppressor with 1 visual detail each.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.6.2 - (Visual Literacy)
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (kind, stern, cold, smile). Sentence frame: 'I see the ________ because she has a ________.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'The stepmother represents the oppressor because...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'How does the visual contrast between Cinderella and her stepmother establish their archetypal roles?'
Differentiation: Provide printed character cards with simplified visual cues for students needing extra support. Advanced students can write a sentence comparing the two characters' expressions.
Visual Symbolism (10 Mins)
i do
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Now, let's look at the fireplace where Cinderella sits. Is it just a place to get warm, or does it represent her internal hope?' Materials: Slide, Discussion Worksheet. Formative Checkpoint: Turn-and-talk check. Expected answer: It represents her hope burning inside. Fast Finisher: Draw a small symbol of hope.
Note: Run the dynamic [3-MINUTE TIMER ANIMATION HERE] on screen to pace socratic talks."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT analyze the fireplace as a symbol of Cinderella's internal hope. Success Criteria: Partner discussion notes explaining what the fireplace represents.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.6.2
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (fire, warm, hope, dark). Sentence frame: 'The fire represents ________.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'Cinderella sits by the fireplace because...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'In what ways does the fireplace serve as a metaphor for Cinderella's internal state?'
Differentiation: Provide a graphic organizer with a pre-drawn fire and lines for writing. Advanced students can write a short paragraph using the word 'extinguished'.
Character Profiling (20 Mins)
we do
Teacher Script
"Say: 'You will now create a profile card for Cinderella. Use the text from Chapter 1 to find her core desire and the obstacles she faces.' Materials: Chapter 1 text, Profile Card template. Formative Checkpoint: Check if students have written a core desire. Expected answer: To have a better life / be happy. Fast Finisher: Create a profile card for the stepsisters."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT create a character profile card using explicit text evidence. Success Criteria: Completed profile card with name, core desire, obstacle, and 1 quote.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.6.1
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (kind, work, dress, hope). Sentence frame: 'Cinderella wants ________ but her stepmother ________.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'Cinderella's main obstacle is...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Analyze how Cinderella's internal traits help her cope with her external obstacles.'
Differentiation: Provide a partially filled profile card for struggling readers. Advanced students can compare Cinderella's traits with her stepsisters' traits.
Prediction Ticket (05 Mins)
assessment
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Before we leave, write your prediction on the exit ticket. What will change Cinderella's life?' Materials: Exit Ticket slips. Formative Checkpoint: Collect and review tickets. Expected answer: A fairy godmother or an invitation. Fast Finisher: Write one question you have about the next chapter."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT predict the catalyst for change based on character desire. Success Criteria: Completed exit ticket with a logical prediction.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.6.1
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (magic, ball, prince, help). Sentence frame: 'I predict ________ will help her.' WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'The catalyst for change will likely be...' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Formulate a hypothesis about how Cinderella's situation will shift based on her desires.'
Differentiation: Allow oral predictions for WIDA 1 students. Provide a choice of three pre-written predictions for students to select and justify.
2

Lexical Activation

Explore

MP3 (Audio Story)

Lexical Activation (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
""
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT guess missing Tier-2 vocabulary words using context clues. Success Criteria: Correctly identifying the mood or meaning of 3 blacked-out words.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.L.6.6 - (Vocab Acquisition
3

Structural Pacing

Explain

Video-1 (Animated Reading)

Introduction & Warm-Up (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: "Good morning class! Let's look at the central question on our Slide. What do you think about..." Materials: Warm-up slide. Checkpoint: Ask students for quick hand signals."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT activate background knowledge related to Day 3 ELA themes. Success Criteria: Participation in active vocabulary warming tasks.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.1 - Text-based introductions
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Sentence frames and word lists provided.
Differentiation: Offer visual alternatives.
Guided Presentation & Modeling (15 Mins)
i do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Now observe carefully how I identify the main shifts..." Materials: Interactive whiteboard slide. Checkpoint: Turn-and-Talk comprehension check."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT comprehend central ELA/ESL focuses of Day 3. Success Criteria: Note-taking or highlighting target text elements.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.3 - Plot interaction and skill building
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Multi-sensory visuals. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph starters.
Differentiation: Provide graphic organizers for scaffolded modeling.
Collaborative Workshop Practice (15 Mins)
we do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Work with your partner. Spend 10 minutes discussing and sorting these answers..." Materials: Graphic organizer worksheet. Checkpoint: Circulate and verify student responses."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT apply analyzing skills in groups of 2-3. Success Criteria: Filled graphic organizers with text citations.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1 - Productive student cooperation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Vocabulary word wall cards.
Differentiation: Group students strategically by complementary WIDA bands.
Independent Evaluation & Exit Ticket (10 Mins)
you do
Teacher Script
"Say: "This is your final independent exit ticket. Write silently for 10 minutes..." Materials: Print workbook slips. Checkpoint: Collect slips to grade for lesson mastery."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT demonstrate independent mastery. Success Criteria: Submitted exit ticket answering ELA skill questions.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.W.7.4 - Standard ELA evaluation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word banks. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph templates.
Differentiation: Extend text guidelines for high-achievers.
4

Conflict Reflection

Explain

Illustrated Story / Chapter Images

Conflict Reflection (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Class, look at the images on the slide. Cinderella experiences both internal and external conflicts. When she smiles despite her sadness, that is an internal struggle. When her stepmother forbids her from going to the ball, that is an external struggle.' Materials: Slide, Graphic Organizer. Formative Checkpoint: Ask students to turn and talk about which conflict is harder"
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT analyze internal vs. external conflict in Cinderella. Success Criteria: Completed conflict identification chart with 1 internal and 1 external conflict.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.6.1 - (Conflict Analysis)
5

Grammar Isolation

Explain

Video-1 & MP3 (Audio Story)

The Freeze-Frame Hook (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: "Good morning class! Let's look at the central question on our Slide. What do you think about..." Materials: Warm-up slide. Checkpoint: Ask students for quick hand signals."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT activate background knowledge related to Day 5 ELA themes. Success Criteria: Participation in active vocabulary warming tasks.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.1 - Text-based introductions
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Sentence frames and word lists provided.
Differentiation: Offer visual alternatives.
Aural Tense Spotting (15 Mins)
i do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Class, listen for our target grammar as the recording plays."
**[MEDIA TRIGGER: PLAY AUDIO STORY MP3]**
Checkpoint: Correct physically if any student misidentifies past continuous."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT comprehend central ELA/ESL focuses of Day 5. Success Criteria: Note-taking or highlighting target text elements.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.3 - Plot interaction and skill building
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Multi-sensory visuals. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph starters.
Differentiation: Provide graphic organizers for scaffolded modeling.
Collaborative Workshop Practice (15 Mins)
we do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Work with your partner. Spend 10 minutes discussing and sorting these answers..." Materials: Graphic organizer worksheet. Checkpoint: Circulate and verify student responses."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT apply analyzing skills in groups of 2-3. Success Criteria: Filled graphic organizers with text citations.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1 - Productive student cooperation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Vocabulary word wall cards.
Differentiation: Group students strategically by complementary WIDA bands.
Independent Evaluation & Exit Ticket (10 Mins)
you do
Teacher Script
"Say: "This is your final independent exit ticket. Write silently for 10 minutes..." Materials: Print workbook slips. Checkpoint: Collect slips to grade for lesson mastery.
Note: Display the interactive [10-MINUTE COUNTDOWN HERE] timer bar to pace drafting sprints."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT demonstrate independent mastery. Success Criteria: Submitted exit ticket answering ELA skill questions.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.W.7.4 - Standard ELA evaluation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word banks. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph templates.
Differentiation: Extend text guidelines for high-achievers.
6

Reality vs. Fiction

Elaborate

Royal Invitation Realia

Introduction & Warm-Up (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: "Good morning class! Let's look at the central question on our Slide. What do you think about..." Materials: Warm-up slide. Checkpoint: Ask students for quick hand signals."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT activate background knowledge related to Day 6 ELA themes. Success Criteria: Participation in active vocabulary warming tasks.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.1 - Text-based introductions
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Sentence frames and word lists provided.
Differentiation: Offer visual alternatives.
Guided Presentation & Modeling (15 Mins)
i do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Now observe carefully how I identify the main shifts..." Materials: Interactive whiteboard slide. Checkpoint: Turn-and-Talk comprehension check."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT comprehend central ELA/ESL focuses of Day 6. Success Criteria: Note-taking or highlighting target text elements.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.3 - Plot interaction and skill building
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Multi-sensory visuals. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph starters.
Differentiation: Provide graphic organizers for scaffolded modeling.
Realia Analysis (15 Mins)
we do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Look at this historical proclamation artifact. Let us analyze who gets access, when, and where. This shows true power stratification!"
Materials: Royal Invitation Slide Document.
Checkpoint: Ask a student to locate the specific clothing rule constraint."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT apply analyzing skills in groups of 2-3. Success Criteria: Filled graphic organizers with text citations.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1 - Productive student cooperation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Vocabulary word wall cards.
Differentiation: Group students strategically by complementary WIDA bands.
Independent Evaluation & Exit Ticket (10 Mins)
you do
Teacher Script
"Say: "This is your final independent exit ticket. Write silently for 10 minutes..." Materials: Print workbook slips. Checkpoint: Collect slips to grade for lesson mastery."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT demonstrate independent mastery. Success Criteria: Submitted exit ticket answering ELA skill questions.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.W.7.4 - Standard ELA evaluation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word banks. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph templates.
Differentiation: Extend text guidelines for high-achievers.
7

Perspective Flip

Elaborate

Illustrated Story / Chapter Images

Perspective Flip (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Class, today we are looking at perspective. We know the stepmother was stern with cold eyes. But later, she was remorseful. Let's think about her silent regrets.' Materials: Slide, Graphic Organizer. Formative Check: Ask students to name one reason the stepmother might feel remorseful. Expected answer: Because she was cruel to Cinderella. Fast Finisher: Write one question you would ask the stepmother."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT analyze character perspectives and identify the concept of an unreliable narrator. Success Criteria: Completed perspective comparison chart.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1 - (Discussion)
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word bank (remorseful, stern, cold, cruel). Frame: 'The stepmother was ______.' WIDA Level 3: 'The stepmother felt remorseful because ______.' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Although she acted with cruelty, her later actions suggest...'
Differentiation: Provide visual cards showing the stepmother's expressions (stern vs. remorseful).
Twisted Tale Synopsis (15 Mins)
i do
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Let's read this short text. We see she was feeling remorseful.' Materials: Text handout. Formative Check: Have students highlight the word 'remorseful'. Fast Finisher: Find a past continuous verb in the text."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT read and analyze a short text about the stepmother's perspective. Success Criteria: Highlighted text showing remorseful feelings.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Highlight key words. WIDA Level 3: Sentence starter: 'She was feeling remorseful when...' WIDA Levels 4-5: Analyze how the shift from stern to remorseful happens.
Differentiation: Audio support for reading.
Role Play Scenarios (15 Mins)
we do
Teacher Script
"Say: 'With your partner, act out the scene where the stepmother begs for forgiveness. Cinderella, show compassion and dignity without hesitation!' Materials: Scenario cards. Formative Check: Listen for the words 'remorseful' and 'compassion'. Fast Finisher: Write down the dialogue you just spoke."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT act out a scene between Cinderella and her stepmother using target vocabulary. Success Criteria: Role-play performance using at least two target vocabulary words.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Simple script: 'I am remorseful.' 'I forgive you with compassion.' WIDA Level 3: 'I am remorseful for my cruelty.' 'I accept your apology with dignity.' WIDA Levels 4-5: Create original dialogue using past continuous.
Differentiation: Pair WIDA 1-2 students with stronger speakers.
Socratic Debrief (10 Mins)
assessment
Teacher Script
"Say: 'Let's discuss. Did the stepmother show real remorse when she arrived at the palace?' Materials: Discussion rubric. Formative Check: Ensure every student contributes or writes a response. Fast Finisher: Write a summary of the debate."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT debate character motivations using text evidence. Success Criteria: One spoken contribution with evidence.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: 'I think she is ______.' WIDA Level 3: 'She showed remorse because she ______.' WIDA Levels 4-5: 'Her actions in Chapter 10 demonstrate that...'
Differentiation: Allow written responses instead of speaking for selective
8

Story Arc Review

Evaluate

MP3 (Audio Story)

Introduction & Warm-Up (10 Mins)
intro
Teacher Script
"Say: "Good morning class! Let's look at the central question on our Slide. What do you think about..." Materials: Warm-up slide. Checkpoint: Ask students for quick hand signals."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT activate background knowledge related to Day 8 ELA themes. Success Criteria: Participation in active vocabulary warming tasks.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.1 - Text-based introductions
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Sentence frames and word lists provided.
Differentiation: Offer visual alternatives.
Guided Presentation & Modeling (15 Mins)
i do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Now observe carefully how I identify the main shifts..." Materials: Interactive whiteboard slide. Checkpoint: Turn-and-Talk comprehension check."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT comprehend central ELA/ESL focuses of Day 8. Success Criteria: Note-taking or highlighting target text elements.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.RL.7.3 - Plot interaction and skill building
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Multi-sensory visuals. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph starters.
Differentiation: Provide graphic organizers for scaffolded modeling.
Collaborative Workshop Practice (15 Mins)
we do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Work with your partner. Spend 10 minutes discussing and sorting these answers..." Materials: Graphic organizer worksheet. Checkpoint: Circulate and verify student responses."
Pedagogical Purpose
SWBAT apply analyzing skills in groups of 2-3. Success Criteria: Filled graphic organizers with text citations.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.SL.7.1 - Productive student cooperation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Vocabulary word wall cards.
Differentiation: Group students strategically by complementary WIDA bands.

you do

SWBAT demonstrate independent mastery. Success Criteria: Submitted exit ticket answering ELA skill questions.
Standards Alignment
CCSS.W.7.4 - Standard ELA evaluation
Scaffolding: WIDA Levels 1-2: Word banks. WIDA Level 3: Paragraph templates.
Differentiation: Extend text guidelines for high-achievers.

i do
Teacher Script
"Say: "Class, begin your digital or written multimedia quiz. Each question runs on an active 12-second countdown timer!"
Materials: Computer projectors, sound system."

SWBAT comprehend central ELA/ESL focuses of Day 10. Success Criteria: Note-taking or highlighting target text elements.
